Neural DSP

Rok-Lok
• 1 set of Rok-Lok strap locks

events.name: Inanna Spring Books Celebration
events.description:
Join Inanna Publications as we celebrate our brand-new books with Toronto authors Mary Lou Dickinson and Mary Frances Coady.

Inanna Publications is excited to celebrate our spring 2027 books with our Toronto-based authors! Join us for good conversations, new friends, great literature, and free snacks.

***

First, listen as Mary Frances Coady reads from As the Morning Rising, the long-awaited companion novel to Holy Rule and The Practie

***

Next, join Mary Lou Dickinson in conversation with Catherine Walker, author of A Watch of Nightingales, as they discuss the trials and joys of publishing later in life.Mary Lou Dickinson's new memoir Late Bloomer traces her journey as a feminist of the Silent Generation, growing up at a time where it seemed impossible. Though she had long published stories in literary magazines, it wasn’t until the age of 70 that her first book was published, proving that it’s never too late to claim one’s voice.

***

Finally, get a sneak peek of our fall lineup with a special advance reading from Sonal Champsee's debut novel, Everyone Can't Be Wrong.

***

We will also have new books from Inanna Publications on sale, including Chimeras by Tegan Zimmerman and One Sunday Without Desire by Cecelia Frey.

events.name: Breaking Sound Canada
events.description:
FEATURING — Bree Bartha, Alyssa Messina, Fafiélla, Jai Vinyl + More!

GET TO KNOW THE ARTISTS:


Bree Bartha is a songwriter based in Toronto, known for her enchanting voice and raw storytelling. Her honey-sweet performances feel like a conversation with a close friend, filled with candor, inside jokes, and vulnerability. Rooted in folk storytelling and R&B sensibility, she’s performed everywhere from cozy cafés to three sold-out nights singing with the KW Symphony, drawing audiences in with unmistakable warmth. Describing her writing style as “live from her bedroom floor,” Bree’s lyrics read like postcards from life’s little moments, and you’ll want to keep every single one.

 

Alyssa Messina, Toronto indie based singer songwriter, known for her latest album release “When the War is Over” is excited to hit the stage again at the Supermarket with Breaking Sound, performing an intimate acoustic set of originals.

 

Fafiélla an alternative soul songstress from Sint Maarten who transforms life's hardships into healing anthems, he weaves her Caribbean roots and Latin childhood influences into a sound uniquely her own. Her music is an intimate journey through vulnerability, observing growth from pain with unflinching honesty.

 

Jai Vinyl Full of sass, a spice of sorrow, and something sweet, Jai Vinyl is an emerging singer-songwriter based in Ontario. Landing somewhere between the brightness of new-wave indie pop and the soft sound of the sunny 70s singer-songwriters, Jai’s strikingly mature lyrics have a way of touching the heart of any listener.

events.name: KOJAK LIVE @ Supermarket
events.description:
Get ready to groove all night with KOJAK at Supermarket - Joined by The Young Scones and Nick Gilmore & The Converts

Kojak is a Canadian indie-rock band built on big hooks, raw emotion, and the kind of live energy that makes small rooms feel enormous. Emerging from Ontario’s vibrant rock scene, Kojak blends driving guitars, melodic bass lines, and dynamic vocals to create a sound that’s both modern and nostalgic — equal parts grit and heart.

After the departure of their former lead guitarist, Kojak found new fire with the addition of a fresh player, pushing their sound into its most inspired era yet. The band is currently in the studio crafting new material that captures who they are right now: tight, loud, honest, and unafraid to hit hard.


Nick Gilmore & The Converts are a dark, moody fusion of alt-country, hard-edged rock and poetry- a distinctive sound that they’ve dubbed Gothic- Americana. Haunting organ, reverb soaked guitars and a chugging acoustic rhythm serve as the perfect foundation for songwriter Nick Gilmore’s lyrics that delve into themes of desolation, spirituality, heartbreak, and salvation. They’ve brought their energetic and raw live performances to many of Toronto’s famed music venues and strive to bring the same organic quality to the studio. To date they have released a four song EP and several singles. Their most recent song ‘First Water Diamond’ is a perfect embodiment of the band’s atmospheric sound and Nick Gilmore’s lyrical artistry, it is available on all streaming services January 16.


Born in the bars and basements of Toronto, The Young Scones have been making a loud, unapologetic noise since early 2022. Fusing razor-sharp riffs, stomping rhythms, and snarling hooks, the band delivers a raw brand of rock ‘n’ roll that’s as infectious as it is unfiltered. Their sound pulls from the glory days of The Beatles, The Clash, The Pixies, and T. Rex, while nodding to the modern grit of BRMC, Jack White, and Arctic Monkeys — all filtered through a distinct Toronto attitude.

events.name: Sorry, Not Sorry by Mark Critch: In Conversation with Mark McKinney
events.description:
Join us for a conversation between Mark Critch + Mark McKinney, followed by audience Q&A.

Penguin Canada presents Mark Critch (This Hour Has 22 Minutes, Son of a Critch), in conversation with Mark McKinney (The Kids in the Hall, Superstore), for the launch of Sorry, Not Sorry: An Unapologetic Look at What Makes Canada Worth Fighting For.

Doors open at 6:30 PM and event begins at 7:00 PM. There will be an hour conversation, including an audience Q&A.

Tickets are FREE and books will be available for sale from Another Story Bookshop. There will be an opportunity to have your book signed following the event.


ABOUT THE BOOK

Known for his sharp-witted skits on CBC’s This Hour Has 22 Minutes, Mark Critch uses his keen observational skills to explore Canadian identity and the new wave of Canadian patriotism.

In Sorry, Not Sorry, Critch delves into the heart of what it means to be Canadian at a time when national pride is on the rise. Examining everything from the historical decision of Newfoundland to join Canada, to the modern-day implications of the Freedom Convoy, and the evolving symbolism of the Canadian flag, he reminds us of where we came from, and of the many reasons Canada is worth fighting for—elbows up!

In chapters like “It Can Happen to You,” “I Want My Flag Back,” and “Operation Trojan Moose,” Critch uses humour and satire while referencing our all-important history to tackle serious questions about national identity and the future of Canada. He offers a unique perspective on the country's relationship with the monarchy, the cultural impact of Canadian celebrities living abroad, and the hypothetical scenarios of provincial separations.

Sorry, Not Sorry is more than just a comedic take on Canadian life; it’s a deeply personal and insightful look at the free country Critch has spent decades satirizing. Whether he’s writing as a lieutenant in a fictional American invasion or penning a love letter to Canada in the voice of Donald Trump, Critch’s essays are sure to entertain, provoke thought, and stir a sense of pride in readers.


ABOUT THE AUTHOR

Mark Critch is a national bestselling author and one of the most recognizable names in Canadian comedy. For twenty-three years he has starred on CBC’s popular This Hour Has 22 Minutes. He adapted his first book, Son of a Critch, into a hit TV series, airing on the CBC in Canada and Netflix in the United States.


ABOUT THE MODERATOR

Mark McKinney has performed in, written, and produced some of the most beloved comedy television in North America. As co-creator, writer, and ensemble cast member of The Kids in the Hall, he was nominated for three Emmy® Awards and multiple Gemini Awards, including Best Writing and Best Performance. McKinney followed the success of his series with three seasons as both a principal cast member and staff writer on Saturday Night Live, and, most recently, starred, and directed on the hit sitcom, Superstore.

Come join us for a night of music at Supermarket! With The Groove Commission, a full 12-piece Soul/Funk ensemble led by Peter Woodward, based in Toronto. Their repertoire spans from 70's classics like Michael Jackson, Tower of Power, and Earth, Wind & Fire to the band's own original compositions, and modern hits from stars like Bruno Mars and Jordan Rakei.

events.name: PROMO A HOMO: EPISODE 3
events.description:
After two sold-out shows, Promo A Homo is BACK — and we’re turning it UP UP UP ! We’ve doubled the capacity, teamed up with QueerMingle, and are taking over Supermarket for a night of laughs, flirting, PowerPoints, and pure queer chaos. Expect 200+ queers, nostalgic beats, and the most unhinged presentations this side of cuffing season. 💅

💻 HOW IT WORKS:
Think dating show meets comedy night meets PowerPoint party.
You’ll create a 3–5 minute slideshow to promote your single friend - tell us why they’re amazing, weird, talented, hot, or totally chaotic. Show off their green flags, hidden talents, or most iconic moments.

🎤 Each presentation ends with audience Q&As for the single.
🏆 Best presentation wins a prize!

💌 WANT TO PRESENT SOMEONE?
There are only 12 presentation spots for SINGLE SAPPHIC/FLINTA 25+ IN AGE . First come, first served!

To apply, email vqtevents@gmail.com with:
  • Your name
  • The name of the person you’re presenting
  • Their age or preferred dating age range
  • The soonest date you can submit your presentation, the quicker you get it in the sooner you can secure your spot and any revisions can be made!
We accept Canva, PowerPoint, or Google Slides — and here’s how to make your presentation stand out:
✨ Add background music or sound effects
🎬 Include a mini video clip or voiceover
🎨 Use dramatic transitions or memes
💖 Tell a short story about your friend (the funnier or more heartfelt, the better!)
💅 Channel your inner game show host, rom-com narrator, or chaotic matchmaker energy

📨 Final presentations are due by October 29th to vqtevents@gmail.com.

🎟️ Presenters + presentees get in FREE!
Everyone else grab a spectator ticket and enjoy the chaos, laughter, and maybe even meet your next crush 👀

🏳️‍🌈 WHO’S THIS FOR?
This round, our presentees (the singles being showcased) will be FLINTA — that’s femme, lesbian, intersex, nonbinary, trans, and agender folks.
✨ But this time, queer men are welcome to attend and present their Sapphics!


Whether you want to hype up your friend, your ex, or find your future situationship , bring your charm and join the fun.

✨ Come for the slides. Stay for the beats if DJ Butt Mitzfa.

Leave with a crush (or three).
This event will sell out fast, so grab your tickets early and follow @veryqueertoronto and @Queermingle for updates, highlights, and chaotic teaser clips.

events.description:
Looking for a fun and unique way to meet other eligible singles? Join us at the legendary Supermarket in Kensington Market for an evening of speed dating followed by live music with some great cover bands!

We will start off the evening with some socializing and mingling, followed by speed dating where you will meet up to 20 other singles. After that, we will hang out and enjoy some amazing cover bands. Your ticket includes speed dating, and admission to the live music concert.

How speed dating works is that you will meet members of the opposite sex for quick 4 minute dates, and then you will give them a yes or a no. They will then do the same thing for you. If there is a mutual match, contact information will be exchanged the next day.

We will give you all needed materials, and explain the process to you once everyone has arrived. You don't need to bring anything, just come dressed for success!

This event is for people aged 35-47. Spots are limited as we have to keep the numbers even, so get your ticket before they are gone!

events.name: THEATRE TRIVIA NIGHT
events.description:
Theatre Trivia Night is back!

Horrorshow Productions, a Toronto-based non-profit community theatre company, is raising funds for their upcoming full-scale musical, Sweeney Todd: The Demon Barber of Fleet Street, running December 5–14 at the Alumnae Theatre.

Join us for a fun evening of trivia featuring a mix of general knowledge and theatre-themed questions. If you’re a theatre fan, love a good challenge or great prizes, this night is for you! We’ll have a big 50/50 draw, and if you attend, you’ll get early access to discounted tickets to Sweeney Todd!

Teams of 4 to 6 players are recommended (minus 1 point for every player over 6). Bring your own team or join one when you arrive.

We’ll have pizza, snacks, and Horrorshow Productions merch—buttons, stickers, and t-shirts—available to support the cause. Enjoy great food, drinks, friendly competition, and a night that gives back to the arts!

All proceeds from trivia tickets, donations, food, merch, and the 50/50 draw go toward our GoFundMe campaign. We’ve already raised 75% of our $10,000 goal, and this event will help us reach the finish line and bring this production to life.

https://www.horrorshowproductions.com/

https://www.instagram.com/horrorshowproductions/

events.name: Summertime Radness
events.description: Step right up, and come on down to Toronto's ulitmate & musically gifted evening courtesy of Percocet Blonde, Friends From Work and The Anytimers. This evening is sure to be packed with with good music, good times and good people. With some of Toronto's hottest up and coming bands, the msuic is almost guaranteed to be better than whatever you are hearing on the radio. Put it this way: if Beethoven and a lightning storm had a child and it grew up binge-listening to vinyl in a smoky jazz lounge before joining a spaceship rock tour, would still only be the opening act "The Anytimers". And it's only just beginning, Freinds from Work, put it this way: This band is like if Orpheus plugged his lyre into a stack of amps, summoned Hades and Poseidon for a bass battle, and turned the Underworld into a sold-out arena. And Percocet Blonde, put it this way: this band is like a gang of time-traveling outlaws who stole the best sounds from every decade, fused them into a a wave of sonic and auditory pleasures, and came to kick them into your ears and chew bubble gum—and they are fresh out of bubble gum.

events.name: TRIED & TESTED presents: TJ Mori extended vinyl set
events.description: This summer at Supermarket, we’re bringing house music back to the dancefloor with a very special extended set by TJ Mori! Get ready for a night that sets the tone for the rest of the season—an unforgettable journey through classic house, raw minimal grooves, and vinyl selections that blend fresh cuts with timeless favorites. Expect high energy, deep rhythms, and a vibe rooted in community and shared moments that last long after the music ends. TJ Mori, the man behind Benice Recordings, blends deep house, minimal, and ambient sounds into stripped-back, hypnotic journeys. A producer first, he's able to build sets and tracks designed for deep listening and late-night floors. With a strong focus on music production, TJ's sonic identity is built from the ground up. Each release reflects a deep commitment to tone, space, and movement — whether it's a slow-burning deep house cut or an experimental club tool. His latest upcoming release Tried & Tested on Benice Recordings, continues to push toward a minimal, soulful aesthetic, raw basslines and classic-Chicago house aesthetic. Though most at home surrounded by synths and drum machines, TJ Mori also brings his signature sound to the dancefloor — delivering DJ sets that are subtle, groovy, and intentionally restrained, creating space for deep listening and connection. Whether you’re a longtime house music lover or just discovering the sound, you’re invited to join us, and take a journey through musical styles eras with TJ Mori. Laugh, dance, and connect with the community of like-minded individuals this June 6 at Supermarket.

events.name: A Late Night with Existential Dread - Hosted by Nicholas Eddie
events.description:
A Late Night with Existential Dread
- Hosted by Nicholas Eddie
An absurd cabaret for a world on fire.

Join us for an unforgettable evening of chaotic comedy, soul-crushing song, daring drag, unhinged desk bits, audience games, a weird man trapped in a room, and special guests grappling with the meaninglessness of existence—live on stage!

Styled like a late-night talk show slowly spiralling into absurdity, A Late Night with Existential Dread is a fundraiser cabaret that brings together some of Toronto’s boldest young artists for a night of catharsis, laughter, and delightful despair.

Featuring:
  • A satirical desk host with questionable credentials
  • Local performers who sing, scream, dance, and make funnies
  • Interactive segments like Spin That Wheel of Dread™
  • A man alone in a room, performing to no one
  • A raffle with prizes to help you cope
All proceeds support ZEITGEIST, a new play by Ben Yoganathan premiering at the 2025 Toronto Fringe Festival, produced by Off The Nose.

Follow us on Instagram for event updates and guest performer announcements: @off.the.nose

events.description:
Dear students and ravers alike... 

Swing by Kensington Market's iconic Supermarket venue for a curated mix of house, garage, drum and bass, and any other rave sound that defines the past four years of university. With a lineup featuring exclusively student DJs from the University of Toronto, this event will be a tasteful showcase of local talent who promise to keep you dancing all night. For only $10 each to cover venue costs, enjoy a final opportunity to connect and dance with your peers in celebration of closing this special chapter in life. As four friends who met back in our first year of studies, we long dreamed of spreading the rave culture and throwing memorable parties for the rest of our year group. With only a matter of weeks left together in Toronto, we are beyond excited to finally make this dream happen. On May 2nd, all of the moments and experiences that we built together over the years will culminate in a final send-off: the 2025 Graduation Rave. Don't miss out!!! Come experience the biggest graduation party of the year - we won't ever get these years back. 

Jack, Matt, Gabe and Raihan.

events.name: Intergalactic Jam Wars
events.description:
The ultimate battle of sound is here! Get ready for an electrifying, interstellar music showdown, where beats collide, creativity takes flight, and the universe pulses with rhythm. This one-of-a-kind event unites musicians in a high-energy jam session that will leave you starstruck. Whether you're here to compete, vibe, or discover new sounds, prepare for an experience that transcends space and time!

This event isn’t just about music—it’s about giving back. SickKids Foundation was chosen as our charity because, for many of us, SickKids isn’t just a hospital—it’s a place of hope. Whether it’s through personal experiences, family, or friends, we’ve seen firsthand the life-saving care, groundbreaking research, and incredible support they provide to children and their families.

Through Intergalactic Jam Wars, we’re helping fund research, treatments, and programs that change lives every day. Supporting SickKids means supporting children who deserve the best possible care, and we couldn’t think of a more meaningful cause to stand behind.

This is more than a fundraiser—it’s a way to give back to a place that has given so much to so many.

events.name: For the Love of Toronto: Talking About Our City
events.description:
Are we as disconnected from each other as people think we are?

Back by popular demand, For the Love of Toronto invites you to explore what it means to love this ever changing world class city. This series of speakers will have honest conversations about how we’re drifting apart as a city, and explore unexpected ways that we may be more connected than you think.

This is a night to explore what it means to love Toronto — the good, the bad, the ugly, and the beauty of it all.

Hosted by CBC Radio’s Elamin Abdelmahmoud (CBC’s Commotion), this event will feature a powerful lineup of voices who are shaping the cultural landscape of our city. Through personal stories, bold ideas, and moments of reflection, these speakers will share their unique perspectives on everything from the arts to nature, from food to design.

Expect an evening filled with insight, laughter, and inspiration as we dive deep into the heart of Toronto. Confirmed speakers include:

Confirmed speakers include:
  • Slam poetry champion, Dwayne Morgan
  • Creative Writer, Emcee, and Co-Founder of R.I.S.E Edutainment, Elle De Lyon
  • Wavelength Music Festival Founder, Jonny Davenport
  • Award-winning graphic novel illustrator and author, Maurice Vellekoop
  • Toronto Zoo CEO, Dolf DeJong
  • Co-Founders of socially-conscious wine events and wine shop, Grape Witches, Nicole Campbell & Krysta Oben
  • Renound comic artist, Ho Che Anderson
  • and more

events.name: 2 Shots Of Hennessy: The Process is the Result
events.description:
Album Launch Show @ Supermarket Toronto

With special guest: Chris Birkett

Friday December 20th

7-10PM, $20 at door


Hey everyone! Dylan here.

In March, me and my dad took a special road trip out to Moncton to record an album with our friend Louis Marc Vatour to tape at his home studio. The trip was an unforgettable experience of bonding over music, friends and family captured in this special record. Now with it finished, we'd like to celebrate the release of "The Process is the Result" the best way we see fit: with an evening of live music.


December 20th we invite you to join us at Supermarket for an evening of music to celebrate our release with special guest: acclaimed songwriter and record producer Chris Birkett.


We look forward to releasing this album on December 9th, and seeing you on the 20th!

events.name: sugarush: Pop Party 2024
events.description:
2024 has been a year of pure pop magic!


We witnessed the meteoric rise of the Midwest Princess, Chappell Roan, and party girl Charli xcx rightfully claim a whole season, aka Brat Summer. 


Sabrina Carpenter had us hooked on her Betty Boop pop, while Billie Eilish had us eating up “Lunch.”


From Lorde & Charli xcx working it out on the remix to Addison Rae becoming a certified hitmaker with “Diet Pepsi,” the year has been packed with iconic moments!


As 2024 wraps up, let’s soak it all in together.


Join us for the final sugarush parties of the year—expect the best pop tracks of the year, fierce drag performances, and themed drinks!


Don’t miss out on your last chance to celebrate pop’s finest with us!
